response.justification: James Smith is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with strong web, TypeScript, and Node.js expertise, but the role demands a deep C++ specialist with 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development, industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443), and IoT/embedded systems experience. His only C++ exposure is a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a thesis project — far short of the seniority required. His career has moved firmly into the web/TypeScript/DLang space, and there is no evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), multi-threaded C++ systems, or SDL practices required for this role.
strengths: Genuine high-performance, low-latency distributed systems experience (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O and caching)
Strong event-driven and micro-services architecture background relevant to the message-broker and scalable-platform aspects of the role
Demonstrated polyglot ability and long track record of delivering production software across diverse stacks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old — 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 does not constitute the 8–12 years of C++/STL expertise required
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or embedded/on-premises platform development
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or any security-engineering practices demanded by the role
